verses.1: A good name is more desirable than great riches,
verses.2: The rich and the poor have this in common:
verses.3: A prudent man sees danger and hides himself;
verses.4: The result of humility and the fear of the LORD
verses.5: Thorns and snares are in the path of the wicked;
verses.6: Train up a child in the way he should go,
verses.7: The rich rule over the poor.
verses.8: He who sows wickedness reaps trouble,
verses.9: He who has a generous eye will be blessed,
verses.10: Drive out the mocker, and strife will go out;
verses.11: He who loves purity of heart and speaks gracefully
verses.12: The LORD’s eyes watch over knowledge,
verses.13: The sluggard says, “There is a lion outside!
verses.14: The mouth of an adulteress is a deep pit.
verses.15: Folly is bound up in the heart of a child;
verses.16: Whoever oppresses the poor for his own increase and whoever gives to the rich,
verses.17: Turn your ear, and listen to the words of the wise.
verses.18: For it is a pleasant thing if you keep them within you,
verses.19: I teach you today, even you,
verses.20: Haven’t I written to you thirty excellent things
verses.21: To teach you truth, reliable words,
verses.22: Don’t exploit the poor because he is poor;
verses.23: for the LORD will plead their case,
verses.26: Don’t you be one of those who strike hands,
verses.27: If you don’t have means to pay,
verses.28: Don’t move the ancient boundary stone
verses.29: Do you see a man skilled in his work?
